name.text: Determining the Centre and Angle of Rotation

questiontext.text:
Triangle \(\triangle A'B'C'\) is the image of triangle \(\triangle ABC\) after a rotation. Use the coordinates provided and the principles of geometric construction to find the transformation details.

  • Original: A({@x1@}, {@y1@}), B({@x2@}, {@y2@}), C({@x3@}, {@y3@})
  • Image: A'{@pA_prime@}, B'{@pB_prime@}, C'{@pC_prime@}
[[jsxgraph width="450px" height="450px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); // Original Triangle var poly1 = board.create('polygon', [{#pA#}, {#pB#}, {#pC#}], {fillColor: 'blue', fillOpacity: 0.2, name: '△ABC', withLabel: true}); // Image Triangle var poly2 = board.create('polygon', [{#pA_prime#}, {#pB_prime#}, {#pC_prime#}], {fillColor: 'red', fillOpacity: 0.2, name: "△A'B'C'", withLabel: true}); [[/jsxgraph]]
  1. Find the coordinates of the centre of rotation (O).
    O = [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]
  2. Find the angle of rotation in degrees.
    Angle = [[input:ans2]]° [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]

(Note: Use negative values for clockwise rotation and positive for anti-clockwise.)

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Step 1: Join corresponding points and construct perpendicular bisectors

Join point C to C'. The perpendicular bisector of the segment \(CC'\) passes through all points equidistant from C and C'.

Step 2: Find the intersection of bisectors

Similarly, join point B to B' and construct its perpendicular bisector. The point where these two bisectors intersect is the centre of rotation, O.

\(\begin{align} O &= ({@cx@}, {@cy@}) \end{align}\)

Constructing a third perpendicular bisector for \(AA'\) would also intersect at the same point \(O\).

Step 4: Measure the angle

Join point C and C' to the centre O. Measure the angle \(\angle COC'\) using a protractor or coordinate geometry.

\(\begin{align} \text{Angle} &= \mathbf{{@ta2@}^\circ} \text{ (Clockwise)} \end{align}\) [[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var b2 = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); b2.create('polygon', [{#pA#}, {#pB#}, {#pC#}], {fillColor: 'blue', fillOpacity: 0.1}); b2.create('polygon', [{#pA_prime#}, {#pB_prime#}, {#pC_prime#}], {fillColor: 'red', fillOpacity: 0.1}); // Centre point var O = b2.create('point', [{#cx#}, {#cy#}], {name: 'O', color: 'black', fixed: true}); // Construction lines for Step 4 b2.create('segment', [O, {#pC#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray'}); b2.create('segment', [O, {#pC_prime#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray'}); b2.create('angle', [{#pC_prime#}, O, {#pC#}], {radius: 1}); [[/jsxgraph]]

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
